descriptions.value:
Sharpshooter:
Patient, deliberate and patriotic.
Gave up rifle sports and her university studies to serve in the military when Germany invaded the Soviet Union. Rapidly established herself as one of the most formidable snipers in the Red Army.

Portrait Painter:
A keen eye and a steady hand. An artist
with a gun. Exchanged brushes and canvas for guns and bullets when subjects stopped sitting
politely and started attacking her. No
previous combat experience but quickly
discovered a talent for dispatching the
undead

Red Army Veteran:
Stoic, practical and no-nonsense. Fiercely loyal to the deserving.
Narrowly avoided charges of insubordination on a number of occasions. He has witnessed and survived far worse than the horrors of the zombie apocalypse.

Academic Researcher:
Former pacifist and sceptic. Would rather be in a library.
Avoided conscription by assisting the Nazis research their favourite obsession: the occult. Inadvertently, he assisted in bringing about the apocalypse. He is no longer a pacifist. Or a sceptic.

Freedom Fighter:
Fierce. Driven by vengeance.
Orphaned at an early age, values family above all else. Wears the uniform of the man who executed her husband and children.

Wehrmacht Captain:
Alert, wary. Doting husband and father. Little patience for politics with its attendant egos and back-stabbing corruption. Never believed in Hitler’s message but followed orders for the sake of protecting his family.

OSS Operative:
Focused and fearless. Unmatched with a sniper rifle. Spent most of the war operating behind enemy lines. Not used to working in a team but willing to adapt if circumstances require it. For example, if confronted by millions of bloodthirsty Zombies.

Resistance Fighter:
Independent and single-minded. Dreams of owning a vineyard. Executed key sabotage missions to facilitate the Allies’ advance through France following the invasion of Normandy. Then travelled alone to Berlin on a personal mission to assassinate Hitler himself.

Quanton is the ruler of all galaxies.
He studies species from the universe.
Quilly is a lonely creature. He lives in an invisible world, mysteriously. Only his house is visible, the rest of his world is made out of gas.
Quilly's House is the only visible thing in the world he lives. The rest of the world is made out of gas.
Quanton's Minions are very fast. They never leave their spaceships, only if Quanton commands it.
Quilly was living a quiet and peaceful life. Now, he's in trouble!
Aliens love the Dubstep. It's the only kind of music they are allowed to listen. "Quanton's order".
This was the first time that Quilly saw another creature in his life.
The first to lay claim to the Aeon Nebula and will defend it at all costs.
Forced to leave their home planet, the Exiles are looking to the Aeon Nebula as a new home.
The Cyborgs require a precious resource that heavily occupies the Aeon Nebula.
The key research vessel for the Cyborg fleet.
This titanic monstrosity has the power to bring down an entire fleet all on its own.
I am the Soul Master.

"Nyrilhothep, the ruler of the desert! Well, at least he carried this title long ago. During the era of Edon, he was good friends with the same-named. It's bizarre that he also saw Raelius as an ally... He denies it till this day! 

Nyrilhothep had been locked up in a tomb, too. He doesn't know who's responsible for this, so he decided to affiliate with the war god's campaign. 

But the desert king isn't as blood thirsty as our master. Quite the opposite: Nyrilhothep was known for being a strict but righteous ruler. He obtained his nation's loyalty by gaining trust, and not by spreading fear. But since the absence of Nyrilhothep, his nation scattered all over the lands. The only thing left are the sands.  "

"Cinda was originally a simple woman from the town Lathisha. She was an stunning beauty! 

But this rose the jealousy of the other villagers, that felt boring and dull compared to her. What started with mobbing and nasty comments eventually ended in an incident: They brought Cinda into the dense Bristlewood, threw her into an old well and sealed it. With a lot of luck, Cinda survived, but had little chance to escape the well. In the very darkness, she managed to prick out a few old stones and open up a tunnel - unaware that she unveiled a spidernest. Following the tunnel for days, she felt spiders crawling all over her body. While she tried not to think about it, she managed not to hurt a single spider. She didn't even wipe them off her face - a behaviour new to the spiders. Thus she managed to earn their trust and the spiders let Cinda to the caves exit, at the other end of the Bristlewood. Even more: By one single, tiny bite, the spiders transformed their powers to her and made her their queen. Since this very day, all spiders did, what she told them to."

"Oh, Raelius! Massive, evil, powerful, cruel war god! The troops of darkness are kneeling down before you! None of your decisions shall ever be questioned! Oh, Raelius, you are... a fool! You won't read this entry anyway. 

My lord - I will have to get used to calling him this - it's sadistical enough to dispel me to write in this dusty library. I should write everything down, so his memories come back! Oh well... I should be glad, though. The other Imps' heads are being cut off on the battlefield while I'm sitting in here and drinking tea. But I have to hide it: Raelius hates tea! 

But there's one thing I appreciate about him: He has got something like integrity. The war gods of Eridia are usually known for their random, insatiable cruelty, but Raelius renounces this and cuts to the chase. Is he a bastard who wants to conquer everything? Sure. But at least he spares his victims unnecessary torment. "

"Herold and Ariella are siblings and both leaders of large armies. They pray to Waron. 

In their childhood, the siblings had to watch their parents being tortured and mutiliated by an insane stranger invading their house. They had troublesome years dealing with what they saw, but they never stopped looking for a reason of this gruelsome deed. 
One day, Waron gave them a mysterious hint: Raelius shall have been responsible for the insanity of the perpetrator! From theron, the siblings knew only one goal: Defeat Raelius and his followers!"

"For centuries, these creatures are known for twisting the humans around their little finger. These fools think they have charm and seductive charisma. Letchett even claims that he had found records as an archaeologist, which proof that muses are literally not of this world, but another planet. 

A likely story! There's proof for other dimensions, but other planets? Don't make me laugh! 

I still have no explanation for Violetta's minor influence on our master, without him even realizing it. Unfortunately, our master doesn't listen to me... Violetta is definitely up to something."

"Mothrilastin is a high priest of the bloodmages and is helping Raelius primarilly because he wants to find out how a god can be created - or how he can become one himself.

As the name suggests, bloodmages are anything but honourable people: They are masters of the dark art of conjuring dark forces from corpses, blood and guts. The gods banished them ages ago - out of reason. In his lust for revenge, Raelius is sympathizing with such beings and decided to get hold of the knowledge of Mothrilastin, even though he does not know much abot him."

"An explorer of endless energy! A cheerful young lad full of charme and wit! 
These traits made archaeologist Letchett famous in his hometown of Lathisha, before Raelius, thankful for his reincarnation, thankfully made him his undead servant. A new role that the skeletal guy does not like at all and that completely turns him around. The formerly cheerful man is now depressive and laconic."
To Protect & Serve. These people put the feet in footwork.
Joe Miller thought his criminal past had been laid to rest. He thought wrong.
Katelyn Hayes may have had some of the top scores at the academy, but will they translate to the real world?
The Kuchka Brotherhood has limited numbers, but one of the tightest criminal hierarchies in the city.
More of a loose collection of different sets, the N13 is the most numerous gang in the city.
Detective Reginald Moore. What's a police to do when retirement sounds fantastic and terrifying at the same time?
Detective Tyrone DeShawn knows all about how a man's past can define his present, and fights it every day.
The old Walton Hotel used to be nice, real nice, but that seems like such a long time ago now.
“You’d best watch my back… I’m already watching yours.”
“We won’t let death impede our journey!”
“We’ll let our fists do the talking!”
“When we work together, we can’t be stopped!”
“Strength comes in many forms!”
“We will bend this land to our will, together!”
